sections.paragraph:
i.q. סוּג to draw back, to depart, only in Kal in the inf. absol. נָסֹוג Isa 59:13 and fut. יִסַּג Mic 2:6.

Hiphil הִסִּיג-

(1) to remove, to take away. Mic 6:14, “thou shalt take away but shalt not save.”

(2) to displace (a landmark) Deu 19:14 27:17 Hos 5:10 once יַשִּׂיגוּ Job 24:2.

Hophal הֻסַּג to be removed, to depart, Isa 59:14. [In Thes. this is merely spoken of as “an uncertain root, see סוּג .”]
